'''Joe Westerman''' (born 15 November 1989) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He played for the [[England Knights]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He has previously played for [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]], and for [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
Westerman was born in [[Pontefract]], [[West Yorkshire]], [[England]].

Westerman grew up supporting [[Castleford Tigers]] and has been a fan of the club since he was 6 years old.

==Club career==
===Castleford Tigers===
Westerman burst onto the scene in 2007 in the [[Castleford Tigers]] [[RFL Championship|National League One]] campaign, having previous played junior [[rugby league]] with [[Castleford Lock Lane]] and the [[Featherstone Lions]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Hull set to unleash secret weapon |url=https://infoweb.newsbank.com/apps/news/document-view?p=AWNB&docref=news/111E5D3A41C6D968 |work=Yorkshire Post |date=27 May 2006 |via=[[NewsBank]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref> Joe played in most of the Castleford Tiger's games and scored 2 tries in the [[RFL Championship|National League One]] Grand Final at Headingley. Joe was named [[RFL Championship|National League One]] young player of the year.
[[File:Joe Westerman.jpg|thumb|right|Westerman playing for the Castleford Tigers in 2008]]
In 2008 Joe had a great first season in the [[Super League]] for Castleford scoring 12 tries in 25 games.<ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=240|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|publisher=thecastlefordtigers|date=31 December 2013|access-date=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120518154227/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=240|archive-date=18 May 2012 }}</ref> He was tipped for being a future England RL international.<ref>{{cite web |title=Scouting report: Joe Westerman, Castleford |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2008/jun/10/superleague.castleford |website=The Guardian |access-date=6 September 2023 |date=10 June 2008}}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
Westerman was [[Hull F.C.]]'s marquee signing for [[Super League XVI]], with the Black and Whites paying a club record fee to secure the services of the former Castleford Tigers man.<ref>{{cite web |title=Joe Westerman joins Hull from Castleford for 'club record' fee |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/oct/28/joe-westerman-hull-transfer-castleford |website=The Guardian |access-date=6 September 2023 |date=28 October 2010}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves===
In October 2015, Westerman signed a 3-year contract with [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] for a fee of £150,000.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-FC-sell-Joe-Westerman-Warrington-150-000/story-28017941-detail/story.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151021120627/http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-FC-sell-Joe-Westerman-Warrington-150-000/story-28017941-detail/story.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=21 October 2015|title=Hull FC sell Joe Westerman to Warrington Wolves for £150,000|date=20 October 2015|newspaper=Hull Daily Mail|access-date=18 February 2017}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

===Toronto Wolfpack===
Upon promotion from [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] in 2017, Westerman signed with Rugby League's first trans-Atlantic team, the Toronto Wolfpack, on 1 October 2017.<ref name="Joe Westerman Joins the Toronto Wolfpack">{{cite web|url=https://www.torontowolfpack.com/2017/10/01/joe-westerman-joins-the-toronto-wolfpack|title=Joe Westerman Joins the Toronto Wolfpack|publisher=Toronto Wolfpack|date=1 October 2017|access-date=1 October 2017}}{{Dead link|date=November 2024 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> Westerman joined fellow Wolves teammate [[Ashton Sims]] for the 2018 season, who signed with the Wolfpack less than a month prior.<ref name="Toronto Wolfpack sign Fiji international prop Ashton Sims for next season">{{cite web|url=https://www.thestar.com/sports/2017/09/13/toronto-wolfpack-sign-fiji-international-prop-ashton-sims-for-next-season.html|title=Toronto Wolfpack sign Fiji international prop Ashton Sims for next season|publisher=Toronto Star|date=13 September 2017|access-date=1 October 2017}}</ref> 

In April 2018 Westerman was released by Toronto Wolfpack after making only 4 appearances for the club. The decision to allow Westerman to leave for free raised eyebrows given he was less than 6 months into a three-year contract and the Wolfpack had paid a transfer fee of £130,000 to secure his services.<ref name="Joe Westerman and Quentin Laulu-Togaga'e leave Toronto Wolfpack">{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11338454/joe-westerman-and-quentin-laulu-togagae-leave-toronto-wolfpack|title=Joe Westerman and Quentin Laulu-Togaga'e leave Toronto Wolfpack|publisher=Sky Sports|date=19 April 2018|access-date=24 May 2020}}</ref>

===Return to Hull F.C.===
Westerman returned to Hull FC in April 2018 despite interest from other clubs. He cited coach [[Lee Radford]] and his love for Hull FC as reasons for his choice.<ref name="Joe Westerman: Hull FC re-sign loose forward from Toronto Wolfpack">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/43838182|title=Joe Westerman: Hull FC re-sign loose forward from Toronto Wolfpack|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=24 May 2020}}</ref>

In the local derby in June 2019 against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], he dislocated his knee in a tackle only to slap it back in place and continue playing.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fcs-joe-westerman-dislocates-17268879|title=Hull FC's Joe Westerman dislocates knee and knocks it BACK IN before playing on in Hull derby|date=27 June 2019 |publisher=Daily Mirror newspaper|access-date=10 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
Westerman signed a two-year deal with [[Wakefield Trinity]] ahead of the [[2020 Super League season]].<ref name="Westermas signs two-year deal">{{cite web|url=https://wakefieldtrinity.com/westerman-signs-two-year-deal/|title=Westerman signs two-year deal|date=24 September 2019|access-date=24 May 2020}}</ref>

=== Return to Castleford Tigers ===
In June 2021, it was announced that Westerman would return to his boyhood club, the [[Castleford Tigers]], on a two-year deal from the 2022 season.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |date=2021-06-22 |title=Joe Westerman: Castleford Tigers to re-sign Wakefield Trinity back-rower from 2022 |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57572807 |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He would be working alongside former coach [[Lee Radford]] once again, as well as playing for the team he supported as a child. Commenting on the news, he said, "The standard is really high now and I just want to get there and play well for my hometown club next year."<ref name=":0" />

Westerman made his second Castleford debut on 11 February 2022 against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-02-11 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 16-26 Salford Red Devils |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60320580 |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He was voted the Fans' Man of the Match for the first two rounds of the season against Salford and [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1493223500944973831|title=After tallying up your votes, Fords faithful you chose @joewesty13787 as the @HuntersHomes Fans Man of the Match following the game against Salford}}</ref><ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1495360081923166210|title=After counting up our supporters' votes, you chose @joewesty13787 as the @HuntersHomes Fans Man of the Match from our match against Warrington. Back-to-back weeks for Westy}}</ref> Following another two Man of the Match performances against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in March, head coach Radford called Westerman "the best 13 in the competition".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Pattison |first=Cam |date=2022-03-26 |title="He's the best 13 in the comp" – Lee Radford praises Joe Westerman and backs another Tiger to make World Cup "statement" |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/hes-the-best-13-in-the-comp-lee-radford-praises-joe-westerman-and-backs-another-tiger-to-make-world-cup-statement-50984/ |access-date=2022-03-28 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> His standout personal year saw him dominate the club's end-of-season awards, being named Directors' Player of the Year, Players' Player of the Year, and Club Player of the Year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=28 September 2023 |title=Westerman signs two-year deal |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8776 |access-date=20 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

In the [[Super League XXVIII|2023 season]], Westerman made 23 appearances for Castleford and scored one try against the [[Leigh Leopards]].<ref name="RLRKC" /> He served as acting captain during the final months of the season due to the injury absence of [[Paul McShane (rugby league)|Paul McShane]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=9 June 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers face anxious wait after Paul McShane suffers suspected broken arm |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-face-anxious-wait-after-paul-mcshane-suffers-suspected-broken-arm-4177623 |access-date=21 November 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> He was named as Players' Player of the Year and Club Player of the Year, retaining the awards for the second consecutive year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=17 September 2023 |title=Tigers host end of season dinner |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8761 |access-date=21 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> In September, Westerman signed a two-year contract extension with the Tigers and said, "I always wanted to end my career at Cas."<ref>{{Cite web |date=29 September 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers: Putt and El-Zakhem join, new contract for Joe Westerman |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66958025 |access-date=21 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=28 September 2023 |title=Westerman signs two-year deal |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8776 |access-date=21 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> Director of rugby Danny Wilson said, "As a person, he's Castleford through and through. He wears his heart on his sleeve when he plays and he's been our best player for the last 2 years."<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=28 September 2023 |title=Joe Westerman ready for key role in Castleford Tigers rebuild after opting to end career at hometown club |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/joe-westerman-ready-for-key-role-in-castleford-tigers-rebuild-after-opting-to-end-career-at-hometown-club-4352992 |access-date=21 November 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

Ahead of the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]], Westerman was appointed Castleford co-captain alongside [[Paul McShane (rugby league)|Paul McShane]] by new head coach [[Craig Lingard]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=11 January 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers coach Craig Lingard appoints co-captains for 2024: 'They know what it means to play for Cas' |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-coach-craig-lingard-appoints-co-captains-paul-mcshane-joe-westerman-for-2024 |access-date=13 January 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=11 January 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers boss Craig Lingard appoints co-captains for 2024 Super League season |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-boss-craig-lingard-appoints-co-captains-for-2024-super-league-season-4474314 |access-date=13 January 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> He scored a try against the [[Salford Red Devils]] on 25 February,<ref>{{Cite web |date=25 February 2024 |title=Super League: Salford Red Devils 26-22 Castleford Tigers - Hosts hold on for first win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337686 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and a second weeks later against the [[Catalans Dragons]] on 16 March.<ref>{{Cite web |date=16 March 2024 |title=Super League: Catalans Dragons 40-14 Castleford Tigers - Fourth win in five games for French side |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68569765 |access-date=17 March 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In December 2024, new head coach [[Danny McGuire]] named Westerman in Castleford's senior leadership group for the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]], with [[Sam Wood (rugby league, born 1997)|Sam Wood]] succeeding both him and the departing McShane as captain.<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=13 December 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers boss Danny McGuire opts for rookie captain in fresh approach |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-boss-danny-mcguire-opts-for-rookie-captain-in-fresh-approach-4909239 |access-date=13 December 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>
Westerman played 23 matches for Castleford in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 11th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/how-castleford-tigers-2025-average-crowd-compares-to-previous-years-with-worrying-dip-after-final-home-game/|title=How Castleford Tigers’ 2025 average crowd compares to previous years with worrying dip after final home game|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

== International career ==
Westerman played for and captained the [[England Knights]] in their 38-18 victory against [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[Leigh Sports Village]] on 15 October 2011.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-10-14 |title=Westerman to lead the Knights |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15314/7240711/westerman-to-lead-the-knights |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

Westerman made his senior international debut on 25 October 2014, starting in [[England national rugby league team|England]]'s 32-26 victory against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at the [[Suncorp Stadium]] in the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hafez |first=Shamoon |date=2014-10-25 |title=Four Nations: England defeat Samoa 32-26 in thrilling opener |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/29700895 |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref name="Four Nations: England international Joe Westerman honoured by Sam Burgess presentation">{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/9530305/four-nations-englands-joe-westerman-honoured-by-sam-burgess-presentation|title=Four Nations: England international Joe Westerman honoured by Sam Burgess presentation|publisher=Sky Sports|date=19 April 2018|access-date=24 May 2020}}</ref>

Westerman made a return to the England team on 25 June 2021, playing against the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-06-26 |title=England 24-26 All Stars: Shaun Wane makes losing start with England |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/england-vs-combined-nations-all-stars/report/55444 |access-date=2022-03-28 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

==Personal life==
In February 2023, Castleford Tigers fined Westerman after an online video was released showing him performing a sex act in public on a woman, who was not his wife. The club also ordered Westerman to help educate young people on the effects of alcohol and "dangers of social media when in the public eye". Westerman issued a statement, saying "I'd also like to apologise to the supporters, sponsors, staff, and directors of Castleford Tigers as well as my team-mates. I'd like to extend this apology to the game of rugby league, and I have realised that I need to work on my decision-making around alcohol."<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64630265|title=Joe Westerman: Castleford Tigers fine back rower over sex act video|website=BBC|date=13 February 2023|access-date=13 February 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby/rugby-league/joe-westerman-video-castleford-b2281489.html|title=Joe Westerman hit with 'substantial fine' over sex act video|website=Independent|date=13 February 2023|access-date=13 February 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-video-joe-westerman-26224318|title=Castleford Tigers issue statement after lewd video of Joe Westerman goes viral|website=Examiner Live|last=Shaw|first=Matthew|date=13 February 2023|access-date=13 February 2023}}</ref>

Westerman is a lifelong supporter of both [[Manchester United]] and [[Castleford Tigers]].
